msgs.text:
Turn 7 — degree 10 dies by a uniform kernel-freedom mechanism I re-read the three current attachments only. The target remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH defined by rS,rH, with F=P⋊G and P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1.The standing goal is exactly to decide whether there is an isomorphism Φ:ΓS→ΓH commuting with the projections to G.

The result of this turn is:The degree-10 residual Maurer–Cartan obstruction also dies.More precisely, after the reductions from Turns 1–6, there is a residual section-twisted lift satisfyingS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI11),and then Turn 6’s odd-layer argument formally clears degree 11 as well. Thus the next genuinely dangerous residual layer is degree 12, not degree 10 or 11.

Recall the residual equation:S(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,Y=1+B,with target X2=1+A2. Turns 3–6 reduced all surviving obstruction tests to this completed mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece, with section twist included; Turn 6 had already reachedS(Y0(9),Y1(9),q(9))≡X2 (modI10),and identified degree 10 as the next possible genuine obstruction because restricted-square/Bockstein terms can occur there.LetG9:=S(Y0(9),Y1(9),q(9))X−2.ThenG9=1+Δ10+O(I11).

Since G9 is group-like, Δ10 is primitive:Δ10∈L10,where L∙ is the free restricted Lie algebra on A,B inside F2⟨⟨A,B⟩⟩.

The degree-10 linear correction map is∂9:L9⊕L9⟶L10,(W,Q)⟼[A,W]+[B,Q],with [U,V]=UV+VU in characteristic 2. The rank calculation is:dimL9=56,dimL10=105,rank∂9=99.So the cokernel has dimension 6. This is exactly the restricted-square quotient:L10/im∂9≅L5[2].In a fixed degree-9 gauge, this looks like a real obstruction. For example, one degree-9-cleared lift has degree-10 square projectione02+e12+e42+e52∈L5[2],with respect to the row-reduced L5 -basise0e1e2e3e4e5=AAAAB+BAAAA,=AAABB+AABBA+ABBAA+BBAAA,=AABBB+ABABB+ABBAB+BABBA+BBABA+BBBAA,=AABAB+AABBA+ABAAB+ABBAA+BAABA+BABAA,=ABBBB+BBBBA,=ABABB+ABBAB+BAABB+BABBA+BBAAB+BBABA.But this is not invariant. The structural reason is that degree 8 corrections used to clear degree 9 have a kernel, and their forced Bockstein tails act nontrivially on precisely this degree-10 square quotient.

Concretely, consider∂8:L8⊕L8→L9,(W,Q)↦[A,W]+[B,Q].

Turn 6 already observed the odd-layer surjectivity at degree 9; here the relevant stronger datum is the kernel:dim(L8⊕L8)=72,rank∂8=56,dimker∂8=16.Using actual restricted group representatives, not naked associative initial forms, the induced map ker∂8⟶L10/im∂9≅L5[2]has rank 6. Hence it is onto.

This is the key uniform statement of the turn:ker∂8↠L5[2].So every possible degree-10 square-cokernel class can be moved away by changing the degree-8 kernel component of the degree-9-clearing correction. This is why the fixed-gauge degree-10 tail is another false obstruction, just like the earlier degree-6 and degree-8 tails. Turns 4 and 5 already warned that single-gauge tails can be misleading once forced Bockstein terms and lower affine freedom are included.After choosing a degree-8 kernel correction that kills the square projection, the remaining degree-10 tail lies in im∂9. A degree-9 correction then kills it. The resulting residual lift satisfiesS(Y0(10),Y1(10),q(10))≡X2 (modI11).Finally, degree 11 is odd. By the Turn 6 primitive-boundary argument,L11=[A,L10]+[B,L10],so once degree 10 is solved, the degree-11 tail is automatically killable by a degree-10 correction. Thus the residual tower is clear through degree 11, i.e. there exists a lift withS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI12).This still does not prove the full over-G isomorphism. It does, however, remove the first even layer where the restricted-square cokernel was genuinely present. The lesson is structural: degree 10 is not solved by testing ∂9 alone; it is solved by the surjectivity of the degree-8 kernel-freedom map onto the square quotient L5[2].

ESTABLISHED: The degree-10 residual Maurer–Cartan obstruction vanishes. More strongly, the degree-8 kernel freedom maps onto the full six-dimensional square quotient L10/im∂9≅L5[2], so every degree-10 square obstruction is movable into im∂9. Together with the odd-layer argument from Turn 6, the residual congruence is clear through degree 11:S(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI12).

GAP: This is still not a full proof that ΓS≅GΓH. The next possible genuine obstruction is degree 12, where the relevant square quotient is L6[2]and where lower interactions such as 4+8, 5+7, and 6+6 must be included.

NEXT: Build the degree-12 residual Maurer–Cartan step and test the uniform analogue ker∂10⟶L12/im∂11≅L6[2].If that map is again onto after including actual restricted/Bockstein representatives, the emerging pattern may become an inductive proof template rather than another finite-layer sweep.[DEG10-SQUARE-COKERNEL-KILLED]
